trips.title: Chile & Bolivia: Deserts, Salt Flats & Otherworldly Landscapes
trips.tagline: From the driest desert on Earth to the world's largest salt flat.
trips.overview: A journey through the Atacama Desert and Bolivia's Uyuni Salt Flats — surreal landscapes, flamingo-dotted lagoons, ancient geysers at 14,000 feet, and some of the clearest night skies on the planet.
highlights: Valle de la Luna sunset hike
3-day jeep tour across the Uyuni Salt Flats
Atacama stargazing — one of Earth's clearest skies

itinerary.title: Arrive Santiago
itinerary.desc: Fly into Santiago. Explore Barrio Lastarria and Cerro San Cristóbal. Book a domestic flight to Calama for morning.

itinerary.title: Santiago → San Pedro de Atacama
itinerary.desc: Fly to Calama, transfer to San Pedro. Afternoon visit Valle de la Luna for sunset among wind-carved rock formations.

itinerary.title: El Tatio Geysers & Atacama Villages
itinerary.desc: 4am departure for El Tatio — steaming geysers at sunrise in freezing altitude. Return via Machuca village for llama empanadas.

itinerary.title: Atacama Lagoons & Stargazing
itinerary.desc: Morning at Cejar and Tebinquinche lagoons (float in salt water). Evening stargazing tour with SPACE observatory.

itinerary.title: Cross into Bolivia
itinerary.desc: Early border crossing. Begin the 3-day jeep tour. Laguna Blanca and Laguna Verde at the foot of Volcán Licancabur.

itinerary.title: Salvador Dalí Desert & Laguna Colorada
itinerary.desc: Surreal rock formations in the Dalí Desert. Afternoon at Laguna Colorada — shallow red lake with thousands of James's flamingos.

itinerary.title: Uyuni Salt Flat
itinerary.desc: Full day on Salar de Uyuni — perspective photos on the infinite white expanse. Visit Isla Incahuasi (cactus island). Sunset on the salt flat.

itinerary.title: Uyuni → Sucre
itinerary.desc: Morning flight from Uyuni to Sucre. Explore Bolivia's constitutional capital — whitewashed colonial architecture, local markets.

itinerary.title: Sucre Day Trip: Maragua Crater
itinerary.desc: Hike the Maragua Crater — Jurassic dinosaur footprints, indigenous weaving villages, dramatic canyon landscapes.

itinerary.title: Sucre → La Paz
itinerary.desc: Fly to La Paz. Ride the Mi Teleférico cable cars for aerial city views. Evening at Mercado Lanza street food stalls.

itinerary.title: La Paz & Death Road
itinerary.desc: Optional: Mountain bike the legendary Yungas Road (Death Road). Or explore the Witches' Market and Valle de la Luna near La Paz.

itinerary.title: Depart La Paz
itinerary.desc: Morning at Tiwanaku ruins (pre-Inca). Afternoon flight home via Santa Cruz or Lima connection.

tips: Altitude is real — acclimatize in San Pedro (2,400m) before going higher
Bolivia visa: US citizens get visa on arrival at border ($160 cash)
Chile is pricier than Bolivia — budget accordingly
The 3-day jeep tour is basic — sleeping bags and shared dorms, but unforgettable
Atacama stargazing is a must — book SPACE or Atacama Desert Stargazing

trips.title: Iceland & Faroe Islands: Volcanoes, Waterfalls & Remote Villages
trips.tagline: A road trip through two of the North Atlantic's most dramatic landscapes.
trips.bestSeason: June – August (midnight sun, all roads open)
trips.overview: Two weeks combining Iceland's Ring Road with a ferry crossing to the Faroe Islands — puffin cliffs, turf-roofed villages, waterfalls that plunge straight into the ocean, and roads that feel like driving through a nature documentary.
highlights: Secret hot springs in the Westfjords

itinerary.title: Arrive Reykjavik
itinerary.desc: Land at Keflavík, pick up 4x4 rental. Explore Reykjavik's harbor, Hallgrímskirkja, and the Sun Voyager. Overnight in Reykjavik.

itinerary.title: Golden Circle
itinerary.desc: Þingvellir National Park (tectonic plates), Geysir geothermal area, Gullfoss waterfall. Overnight near Selfoss.

itinerary.title: South Coast
itinerary.desc: Seljalandsfoss (walk behind the waterfall), Skógafoss, black sand beach at Reynisfjara. Overnight Vík.

itinerary.title: Glacier Lagoon
itinerary.desc: Drive to Jökulsárlón Glacier Lagoon — icebergs floating to sea. Diamond Beach across the road. Zodiac boat tour among the ice.

itinerary.title: East Fjords
itinerary.desc: Scenic drive through the East Fjords — narrow roads, fishing villages, wild reindeer. Overnight Egilsstaðir.

itinerary.title: Mývatn & North
itinerary.desc: Lake Mývatn — pseudocraters, lava formations, Námaskarð mud pots. Evening soak at Mývatn Nature Baths.

itinerary.title: Akureyri & Whale Watching
itinerary.desc: Iceland's northern capital. Whale watching in Eyjafjörður (humpbacks almost guaranteed in summer). Overnight Akureyri.

itinerary.title: Snæfellsnes Peninsula
itinerary.desc: Drive west to Snæfellsnes — Kirkjufell mountain, Arnarstapi sea cliffs, Djúpalónssandur black pebble beach. Overnight Stykkishólmur.

itinerary.title: Ferry to Faroe Islands
itinerary.desc: Drive to Seyðisfjörður (east). Board the Smyril Line ferry (overnight crossing to Tórshavn). Bring snacks — it's a proper North Atlantic sailing.

itinerary.title: Tórshavn
itinerary.desc: Explore the world's smallest capital — turf-roofed Tinganes parliament, colorful harbor, KOKS restaurant (book months ahead). Overnight Tórshavn.

itinerary.title: Vágar — Múlafossur & Lake Above the Ocean
itinerary.desc: Drive to Vágar. Hike to the iconic Múlafossur waterfall plunging into the sea. Sørvágsvatn — the lake that appears to hover above the ocean.

itinerary.title: Mykines — Puffin Island
itinerary.desc: Ferry to Mykines (book early, weather-dependent). Walk to the lighthouse through thousands of nesting puffins. One of the most magical wildlife experiences in Europe.

itinerary.title: Northern Islands
itinerary.desc: Drive to Viðoy and Borðoy — the village of Múli (population: 0-4), Klaksvík's dramatic setting between mountains. Overnight in Klaksvík or Tórshavn.

itinerary.title: Depart Faroe Islands
itinerary.desc: Morning flight from Vágar Airport to Copenhagen or direct to Keflavík. Or take the ferry back for another night at sea.

tips: Book the Smyril Line ferry months ahead — limited car spaces
Mykines ferry cancels in bad weather — keep a backup day
In Iceland, F-roads require a 4x4 and are unbridged rivers
Faroe Islands: tunnels between islands are incredible (some sub-sea)
Pack layers for both — weather changes every 15 minutes

trips.title: Southeast Asia: Temples, Street Food & River Towns
trips.tagline: Thailand, Laos, and Cambodia — three countries, one unforgettable route.
trips.overview: A classic Southeast Asia loop — starting in Bangkok's street food alleys, heading north to Chiang Mai's temples, crossing into Laos via slow boat down the Mekong, and ending at sunrise over Angkor Wat.
highlights: Chiang Mai temples & Sunday night market
Two-day slow boat down the Mekong to Luang Prabang

itinerary.title: Arrive Bangkok
itinerary.desc: Land at Suvarnabhumi. Check in near Khao San or Silom. Evening street food tour in Chinatown (Yaowarat Road) — pad thai, mango sticky rice, grilled seafood.

itinerary.title: Bangkok Temples & Markets
itinerary.desc: Wat Pho (Reclining Buddha), Grand Palace, Wat Arun at sunset. Afternoon at Chatuchak Weekend Market or Jim Thompson House.

itinerary.title: Bangkok → Chiang Mai
itinerary.desc: Morning flight to Chiang Mai. Explore the Old City moat area. Afternoon at Doi Suthep temple with panoramic city views.

itinerary.title: Chiang Mai
itinerary.desc: Morning at an ethical elephant sanctuary. Afternoon cooking class — learn Thai green curry and tom yum from scratch. Evening at the Sunday Walking Street market.

itinerary.title: Chiang Rai Day Trip
itinerary.desc: White Temple (Wat Rong Khun), Blue Temple, Black House. Return to Chiang Mai or overnight in Chiang Rai.

itinerary.title: Chiang Rai → Huay Xai (Laos Border)
itinerary.desc: Bus to the Thai-Lao border at Chiang Khong/Huay Xai. Cross the Mekong. Board the slow boat for tomorrow.

itinerary.title: Mekong Slow Boat Day 1
itinerary.desc: All-day journey down the Mekong — jungle-clad banks, riverside villages, cold Beer Lao. Overnight in Pak Beng (basic but atmospheric).

itinerary.title: Mekong Slow Boat Day 2 → Luang Prabang
itinerary.desc: Second day on the river. Arrive Luang Prabang by late afternoon. Evening at the famous night market along Sisavangvong Road.

itinerary.title: Luang Prabang
itinerary.desc: 5:30am alms-giving ceremony. Royal Palace Museum. Afternoon at Kuang Si Waterfalls — swim in turquoise pools. Evening cocktails on the Mekong.

itinerary.title: Luang Prabang → Vang Vieng
itinerary.desc: Scenic drive (or minivan) through mountains to Vang Vieng. Afternoon kayaking or tubing on the Nam Song River.

itinerary.title: Vang Vieng → Vientiane
itinerary.desc: Drive to the Lao capital. Patuxai arch, Pha That Luang golden stupa. Riverside sunset beers.

itinerary.title: Vientiane → Bangkok → Siem Reap
itinerary.desc: Fly to Bangkok, connect to Siem Reap (or direct flight). Settle into a hotel near Pub Street. Evening fish amok dinner.

itinerary.title: Angkor Wat Sunrise & Main Temples
itinerary.desc: 4:30am wake-up for sunrise at Angkor Wat — the reflection in the moat is iconic. Spend the day: Bayon (face towers), Ta Prohm (jungle temple from Tomb Raider).

itinerary.title: Angkor — Outer Temples
itinerary.desc: Rent a tuk-tuk for the Grand Circuit. Banteay Srei (intricate pink sandstone), Preah Khan, Neak Pean. Less crowded, equally stunning.

itinerary.title: Siem Reap Free Day
itinerary.desc: Floating village on Tonlé Sap Lake, Angkor National Museum, or cooking class. Night: Phare Cambodian Circus (incredible acrobatics).

itinerary.title: Siem Reap → Phnom Penh
itinerary.desc: Bus or fly to Phnom Penh. Royal Palace and Silver Pagoda. Central Market (Art Deco gem). Rooftop sunset drinks.

itinerary.title: Phnom Penh
itinerary.desc: Tuol Sleng Genocide Museum and Killing Fields — difficult but essential. Afternoon at the riverside promenade. Evening at a local Khmer restaurant.

itinerary.title: Depart Phnom Penh
itinerary.desc: Morning flight home via Bangkok, Singapore, or direct to your next destination.

tips: Get local SIMs at each country's airport — cheap and essential
Thailand visa-free 30 days, Laos visa on arrival ($40 cash), Cambodia e-visa ($30)
The Mekong slow boat is unforgettable but basic — bring snacks and a cushion
Angkor pass: 3-day is best value ($62) — you'll want at least 2 full days
Street food is safe and incredible — follow the crowds, eat where locals eat
